articles.title: A cafe is on its way to the Esplanade as a $24 million pavilion project nears completion
articles.description:
After more than two decades of planning, a corner of the Charles River Esplanade near the Museum of Science is expected to reopen this fall, transforming a once-vacant former pool site into a year-round public hub. The $24 million Charlesbank and Smith Family Pavilion has been funded entirely through private donations in what has been […]

The post A cafe is on its way to the Esplanade as a $24 million pavilion project nears completion appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Construction of the $24 million Charlesbank and Smith Family Pavilion nears completion this fall.
The pavilion will transform a vacant pool site into a year-round public hub for residents.
Funding came entirely from private donations, enhancing community resources along the Esplanade.

articles.title: ‘Let us in’ chants echo outside Brigham and Women’s Hospital as nurses strike becomes lockout
articles.description:
